intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers organic, rounded chart aesthetics over sharp technical graphs—suggesting a preference for warm, approachable atmosphere over cold precision
facade.label: Soft data visualizations
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use curved lines, soft gradients, and natural color palettes to feel inviting rather than sterile
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should favor clean grids, sharp lines, and high-contrast elements to feel trustworthy and technically rigorous

facade.hypothesis: Animated chart entries reduce cognitive load by showing data arrival rather than instant appearance
facade.axisTargeted: Fluid animated transitions vs instant static rendering
facade.label: Smooth slide-in charts
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers motion to guide attention and ease data comprehension in health dashboards
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers instant, snappy rendering where charts appear complete immediately without animation

evidence.hypothesis: The user prefers organic, rounded chart aesthetics over sharp technical graphs—suggesting a preference for warm, approachable atmosphere over cold precision
evidence.implication: Dashboard should use curved lines, soft gradients, and natural color palettes to feel inviting rather than sterile

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer conversational, lightweight tone over clinical data entry language
facade.label: Casual check-ins
facade.acceptImplies: Build warm, encouraging language ('How's your sleep been?' vs 'Log sleep duration'). Use emojis selectively. Celebrate small wins.
facade.rejectImplies: Lean toward clinical precision ('Sleep Duration: 7.2 hours'). Neutral terminology. Data-first framing.

facade.hypothesis: A persistent left sidebar navigation paired with card-based layouts for experiment entries will provide fast access to different health metrics while keeping individual experiments visually distinct and scannable.
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure × Content grouping (sidebar vs tabs) × (cards vs lists)
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick navigation to different experiment types and prefers chunked, contained content blocks over linear lists.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer full-width layouts, tab-based navigation, or list-based density for faster scrolling through many experiments.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a single highlighted metric with a one-sentence trend over dense tables of historical data
facade.axisTargeted: Numbers vs narrative — whether the dashboard leads with quantified results or contextual explanation
facade.label: Big number, small story
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should surface one key metric per experiment prominently, paired with plain-language interpretation (e.g., '7.2 miles/week — up 12% since last month')
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should prioritize comprehensive data tables or multi-metric grids, letting users draw their own conclusions from raw numbers

facade.hypothesis: A health dashboard benefits from gentle, approachable forms rather than hard edges — users feel less clinical and more supported when tracking personal data
facade.label: Soft rounded cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore curved containers, rounded buttons, smooth corners throughout the interface to reduce visual friction and create psychological comfort
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer structured clarity with defined boundaries; explore crisp rectangles, grid lines, and sharp corners for a more analytical, no-nonsense feel

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: The user may prefer low-light, calming interfaces that reduce eye strain during frequent health tracking sessions—extending the 'warm and organic' preference into a nocturnal, intimate atmosphere
facade.axisTargeted: Warm vs Cool + Calm vs Energetic
facade.label: Dark mode
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should default to dark backgrounds with soft accent colors; nighttime health logging should feel like a private, reflective ritual rather than clinical monitoring
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers bright, high-contrast interfaces that feel energizing and clear—may lean toward clinical precision over atmospheric warmth; tracking health data should feel active and visible, not tucked away

draft.title: Experiment Tracker Dashboard
draft.summary: A personal health dashboard for monitoring long-term experiments with visual progress tracking, experiment cards, and key metrics at a glance.
